subject: [DCAE] Bump up to Dashboard 1.4.3
commit_message:
[DCAE] Bump up to Dashboard 1.4.3

Fixes login issue introduced by commom/pg version upgrade 
to crunchy-postgres:centos8-13.2-4.6.1
Modified readiness config to match master

Change-Id: I0a5b25c03c25c42987fc2fd0397378250628147c
Signed-off-by: vv770d <vv770d@att.com>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2751
Signed-off-by: vv770d <vv770d@att.com>

Patch Set 3:

https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//121092-3/index.html
only error in Holmes and Policy HC but all smoke tests are OK
As Holmes and Policy (timeout) are somehow linked to DCAE, their deployment seem to be OK according to onap-k8s, so if Jim or Fu could have a look

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//121092-3/index.html
> only error in Holmes and Policy HC but all smoke tests are OK
> As Holmes and Policy (timeout) are somehow linked to DCAE, their deployment seem to be OK according to onap-k8s, so if Jim or Fu could have a look

Policy is not dependent on DCAE, unless DCAE is somehow flooding Policy with requests.  The Enhanced test timed out after 20 seconds, 18 of which were consumed in the first request, Create Policy, which apparently didn't leave quite enough time for the second test, Get Policy.  During today's daily run, the first test only took 8 seconds.

Patch Set 3:

> Policy is not dependent on DCAE, unless DCAE is somehow flooding Policy with requests.  The Enhanced test timed out after 20 seconds, 18 of which were consumed in the first request, Create Policy, which apparently didn't leave quite enough time for the second test, Get Policy.  During today's daily run, the first test only took 8 seconds.

I would suggest changing the timeout for the enhanced Policy test from 20s to 30s.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Policy is not dependent on DCAE, unless DCAE is somehow flooding Policy with requests.  The Enhanced test timed out after 20 seconds, 18 of which were consumed in the first request, Create Policy, which apparently didn't leave quite enough time for the second test, Get Policy.  During today's daily run, the first test only took 8 seconds.
> 
> I would suggest changing the timeout for the enhanced Policy test from 20s to 30s.

Can confirm HC failure on Policy/Holmes are not related to this patch...:).. The readines/timeout can be increased for these components if issues is seen on other gating runs also.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
